title: Provide Context in a Project Initiation Document (PID) – Step 1
summary: Learn how to capture business drivers, high‑level goals, and a clear mission statement in the Context section of a PID to align stakeholders and guide project success.
content: The Context section explains why the project is being undertaken. Include the primary business drivers, the specific problem or opportunity, technical or strategic motivations, high‑level goals, success criteria, and measurable metrics. A concise mission statement helps align the team and keeps strategic objectives visible throughout the project lifecycle.
can_answer: What should be included in the Context section of a Project Initiation Document?
examples: "The client aims to reduce checkout abandonment by 20 % through a streamlined mobile experience."
warnings: Omitting strategic context can lead to misaligned deliverables later.
